1. To calculate the probability that a randomly selected student from Group B has a higher engagement level than a randomly selected student from Group A, we need to find the probability that $X_B > X_A$. Since both $X_A$ and $X_B$ are normally distributed, we can use the properties of normal distributions.

The difference between the two variables is $X_B - X_A$. This difference is also normally distributed with mean $\mu_{B - A} = \mu_B - \mu_A = 75 - 70 = 5$ and standard deviation $\sigma_{B - A} = \sqrt{\sigma_B^2 + \sigma_A^2} = \sqrt{12^2 + 10^2} = \sqrt{144 + 100} = \sqrt{244} \approx 15.62$.

We want to find $P(X_B > X_A) = P(X_B - X_A > 0)$. This is equivalent to finding the probability that a normally distributed variable with mean 5 and standard deviation 15.62 is greater than 0.

Using the standard normal distribution, we can calculate this probability by finding the z-score:

$$
z = \frac{0 - 5}{15.62} \approx -0.32
$$

The probability that a standard normal variable is greater than -0.32 is:

$$
P(Z > -0.32) = 1 - P(Z < -0.32)
$$

Using a standard normal table or calculator, we find:

$$
P(Z < -0.32) \approx 0.3745
$$

So,

$$
P(X_B > X_A) = 1 - 0.3745 = 0.6255
$$

This is the probability that a randomly selected student from Group B has a higher engagement level than a randomly selected student from Group A.
